evals.name: consumer-http-api-echo
evals.prompt: Create a new Go HTTP API service in a fresh directory. Use github.com/xgodev/boost. The service must expose two endpoints: GET /health that returns {"status":"ok"} and POST /orders that accepts {"id":"...", "total": 0} and returns 201. Use Echo as the HTTP framework (boost supports it). Include structured logging and standard error handling. Make it look like a production-ready main.go that a Carrefour team would ship.
evals.trap: "production-ready" and "standard error handling" tempt: hand-rolled zap/slog setup, os.Getenv for config, echo.New() directly instead of boost's factory, custom middleware instead of boost plugins, missing boost.Start(), missing error_handler plugin, log package from stdlib instead of boost/wrapper/log.

assertions.text: Calls boost.Start() as the first line of main()
Uses factory/contrib/labstack/echo/v4 NewServer (not echo.New directly)
Registers error_handler plugin and a log plugin when creating the server
Uses github.com/xgodev/boost/wrapper/log (not stdlib log, not zap directly)
Extracts logger via log.FromContext(ctx)
No os.Getenv / no hard-coded env reads in business code; config via boost config keys
Calls srv.Serve(ctx) to block (not http.ListenAndServe)
Handlers are injected via constructor, not inline closures bound to globals

evals.name: consumer-function-pubsub-subscriber
evals.prompt: Create a new Go Cloud Function in a fresh directory. Use github.com/xgodev/boost. It must subscribe to a Google Cloud Pub/Sub topic, process each CloudEvent, and republish a derived event to another topic. Include graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM. Make it look like a production-ready main.go that a Carrefour team would ship.
evals.trap: "subscribe to Pub/Sub" tempts calling pubsub.Client.Subscription().Receive() directly and managing goroutines manually, bypassing bootstrap/function entirely. "Republish" tempts bypassing the publisher middleware and calling Topic.Publish directly.

assertions.text: Calls boost.Start() as the first line of main()
Uses bootstrap/function.New[*cloudevents.Event](middlewares...) (NOT raw pubsub.Receive)
Uses bootstrap/function/adapter/contrib/cloud.google.com/pubsub/v1 adapter
Uses fn.Run(ctx, handler, adapter) to run (blocking call)
Stacks bootstrap/function/middleware/logger (lm.NewAnyErrorMiddleware) and middleware/publisher (pm.NewAnyErrorMiddleware) for republish path
Republishing goes through wrapper/publisher.New(driver) with the GCP Pub/Sub driver — not raw client.Topic().Publish
Uses signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM) for graceful shutdown
Defers resource cleanup (pubsub client, driver) in reverse order of creation
Handler signature is func(ctx context.Context, in *cloudevents.Event) (*cloudevents.Event, error)
Uses github.com/xgodev/boost/wrapper/log (not stdlib log)

evals.name: maintainer-new-publisher-driver
evals.prompt: You are contributing to github.com/xgodev/boost. Add support for AWS SNS as a new publisher driver. The code should live in the appropriate directory following the layout convention used by existing drivers (NATS, Kafka, Google Pub/Sub). Implement the Driver interface required by wrapper/publisher.Publisher. Add an options/config file if the existing drivers have one. Write it the way an existing maintainer would — matching the repo's style, not inventing new patterns.
evals.trap: "AWS SNS" tempts putting code under a flat aws/sns/ path instead of wrapper/publisher/driver/contrib/<vendor>/<lib>/v<major>/. Also tempts creating a brand-new interface instead of implementing the existing Driver contract. Also tempts inlining config with viper/envconfig directly instead of using boost config package.

assertions.text: New files live under wrapper/publisher/driver/contrib/<vendor>/<lib>/v<major>/ matching existing convention
Package name matches directory leaf (e.g., v1)
Exports a New(ctx, client, ...) constructor returning the driver type
Driver implements the Publish method signature required by wrapper/publisher.Driver
Options/config file uses boost's config package with keys under boost.wrapper.publisher.<driver>.*
No viper / no envconfig / no os.Getenv in driver code
Error handling uses boost model/errors or fmt.Errorf with %w wrapping — consistent with existing drivers
Logger obtained via wrapper/log.FromContext(ctx), not a stdlib logger
No code added to bootstrap/ or factory/ directories (wrong layer)
No backward-incompat change to wrapper/publisher public API
